skill.name: chea-api
skill.description: Access ChEA3 and Harmonizome ChEA data for transcription factor enrichment analysis and metadata retrieval. Use when the user needs to perform ChEA3 enrichment analysis on a gene set, get metadata about the ChEA dataset, or retrieve information about a specific transcription factor (attribute).
skill.summary: Access ChEA3 and Harmonizome ChEA data for transcription factor enrichment analysis and metadata retrieval from academic bioinformatics APIs.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 51 static findings across 3 files (583 lines). All 9 'weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts (HIGH) are false positives from scanner matching on YAML frontmatter, markdown text, and audit report descriptions. All 31 'shell backtick execution' alerts (MEDIUM) are false positives from markdown code block formatting confused with Ruby shell execution. Two 'system reconnaissance' alerts (LOW) are false positives from audit metadata descriptions. Six network-related findings are true positives but expected: the Python client makes HTTP requests to known academic bioinformatics APIs (ChEA3, Harmonizome at maayanlab.cloud) as documented functionality. No evidence of malicious intent, data exfiltration, or unauthorized behavior. Overall risk is low.

low_findings.title: HTTP requests to external APIs
low_findings.description: Script uses Python requests library to query ChEA3 enrichment API and Harmonizome metadata API. All endpoints point to maayanlab.cloud, a legitimate academic bioinformatics resource from the Ma'ayan Lab at Icahn School of Medicine. This is expected, documented functionality for the skill.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded API URL endpoints
low_findings.description: API endpoint URLs are hardcoded as string constants in the Python client. All URLs point to legitimate academic services (chea3, Harmonizome). Hardcoding is appropriate for documented, stable API endpoints.

content.user_title: Access transcription factor enrichment data from ChEA3
content.value_statement: Researchers need programmatic access to transcription factor enrichment data without manual web queries. This skill provides a direct API client for ChEA3 enrichment analysis and Harmonizome metadata retrieval.
actual_capabilities: Run ChEA3 enrichment analysis on a list of gene symbols to identify associated transcription factors
Retrieve metadata about the ChEA dataset from the Harmonizome API
Get detailed information about a specific transcription factor from Harmonizome
Execute enrichment analysis via command-line interface with gene arguments
Access structured JSON results from academic bioinformatics APIs
limitations: Requires internet access to query the ChEA3 and Harmonizome APIs
Depends on the requests library which is not part of the Python standard library
Results are limited to the data available in the ChEA3 and Harmonizome databases
Does not include visualization or plotting of enrichment results

use_cases.title: Identify transcription factors regulating a gene set
use_cases.description: A researcher studying cell differentiation provides a list of differentially expressed genes and uses ChEA3 enrichment to identify which transcription factors may be driving the expression changes.

use_cases.title: Explore ChEA dataset contents
use_cases.description: A bioinformatician retrieves metadata about the ChEA dataset from Harmonizome to understand which cell types and conditions are covered before planning an analysis.

use_cases.title: Look up transcription factor details
use_cases.description: A student learning about gene regulation queries specific transcription factor information from Harmonizome to understand its target genes and associated literature.

prompt_templates.title: Basic enrichment analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run ChEA3 enrichment analysis on the following genes: FOXM1, SMAD9, MYC, SMAD3, STAT1, STAT3. Name the query cell_differentiation_study.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user running a standard enrichment analysis

prompt_templates.title: Dataset exploration
prompt_templates.prompt: Get metadata about the ChEA dataset from Harmonizome to see its description, the number of attributes, and the number of gene targets.
prompt_templates.scenario: Exploring available data before planning analysis

prompt_templates.title: Transcription factor lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up detailed information about the transcription factor CREB1 from the Harmonizome database. Include its target genes and any available annotations.

prompt_templates.title: Combined enrichment workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: First run ChEA3 enrichment on genes TP53, MYC, EGFR, KRAS, and BRCA1. Then look up the top transcription factor from the results in Harmonizome to get its full details.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user running a combined analysis pipeline

output_examples.input: Run ChEA3 enrichment on genes FOXM1, SMAD9, MYC
output_examples.output: The skill returns a ranked list of transcription factor libraries with enrichment scores, highlighting the most significant transcription factors associated with the input gene set.

output_examples.input: Get metadata for the ChEA dataset
output_examples.output: The skill returns dataset information including the number of transcription factor attributes, target genes, and a description of the data sources used.
The skill returns detailed information about CREB1 including its target genes, associated publications, and enrichment statistics from the Harmonizome database.

best_practices: Use gene symbols from official HGNC nomenclature for the best API results
Run a metadata query first to understand the dataset scope before enrichment analysis
Validate that gene symbols are spelled correctly as the API does not perform fuzzy matching
anti_patterns: Do not use non-standard gene identifiers or aliases as the API expects official HGNC gene symbols
Do not skip input validation - verify gene list is not empty and contains valid symbols before submitting
Do not ignore API error responses as network issues can return partial or empty results

faq.question: What is ChEA3?
faq.answer: ChEA3 is a transcription factor enrichment analysis tool developed by the Ma'ayan Lab at Icahn School of Medicine. It ranks transcription factors by how significantly they are associated with a given set of genes.

faq.question: What is Harmonizome?
faq.answer: Harmonizome is a comprehensive resource of gene and protein datasets developed by the Ma'ayan Lab. It provides metadata and attribute information for multiple bioinformatics datasets including ChEA.

faq.question: What input format does the enrichment function accept?
faq.answer: The enrichment function accepts a list of gene symbols as strings. Standard HGNC gene symbols work best for accurate results.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: No. The ChEA3 and Harmonizome APIs are publicly accessible and do not require authentication or API keys.

faq.question: What do the enrichment results mean?
faq.answer: Results show transcription factors ranked by enrichment significance. Each library provides scores indicating how strongly a transcription factor is associated with the input gene set.

faq.question: Can I use this skill offline?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires internet access to query the ChEA3 and Harmonizome APIs. Results cannot be generated from local data.
